belongs to Trotsky. Such too was the case with Queen Victo
ria. All these were exceptionally alert to their responsibilities
and were interested in all these things with a child-like in
genuousness. And such too were Bismarck, Havelock Ellis and
Shakespeare. But when this distance is less than fourteen
degrees, the mind is no doubt eager but controlled or censored
by the will or self. Such a thing we find* in Luther. Here
tradition and conscience play a leading role.
If Mercury rises behind the Sun, that is, when Mercury is
the evening star, and maintains a distance of more than four
teen degrees from the Sun, the mind is deliberate and without
sensitiveness. The mind is more careful and pays attention to
the general store of traditional human wisdom. Sir William
Hamilton was born while Gemini was rising; the Sun (11) Leo,
Venus (29) Leo, Mercury-Virgo (1), Mars (3) Saturn (12) and
Uranus (18) all in Libra, Jupiter (26) and Neptune (26) in
